channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel blocks humanitarian aid into Gaza after Hamas rejects ceasefire extension proposal
item.description: Israel has cut all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip and warned Hamas it would face "additional consequences" if it does not accept a new proposal for an extended ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope stable and resting in hospital, again skips Sunday noon blessing as he recovers from pneumonia
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in stable condition and resting on Sunday as he continued his recovery from double pneumonia, but again skipped his weekly noon blessing to avoid even a brief public appearance.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House row with Ukraine raises stakes for European summit in London
item.description: A summit of European leaders to discuss Ukraine's defense has been overshadowed by the diplomatic row at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan’s volunteer military shrinks amid growing Chinese aggression
item.description: Taiwan's military continues to struggle with recruiting troops as it looks to new methods to boost troop strength. One such idea is to enlist migrants into the island's armed forces.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's military is told to prepare to defend a Druze community outside Syria's capital
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense ministry on Saturday said the military has been instructed to prepare to defend a Druze settlement in the suburbs of Damascus, asserting that the minority it has vowed to protect was “under attack” by Syrian forces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kurdish PKK militants declare a ceasefire in 40-year insurgency in Turkey
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Kurdish militants who have waged a 40-year insurgency in Turkey declared a ceasefire on Saturday in what could mark a significant boost to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's government, two days after their imprisoned leader called for the group to disarm.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey's Kurds wary of path to peace after PKK declares ceasefire
item.description: DIYARBAKIR, Turkey (AP) — A momentous ceasefire declaration on Saturday stirred a mix of emotions in southeast Turkey and northern Iraq, where people bore the brunt of the 40-year conflict between Kurdish militants and the Turkish state.
item.dc:creator: MUCAHIT CEYLAN and ADNAN AHMAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP governors rush to Trump's defense after ‘jarring’ spat with Zelensky
item.description: Red state governors were quick to throw their support behind President Trump following a tense back-and-forth with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the Oval Office Friday afternoon, citing Trump's dedication to protecting Americans.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Namibian founding president Sam Nujoma is laid to rest and praised as the last African liberator
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — Namibia’s founding president, Sam Nujoma, who steered the country to freedom from apartheid South Africa, was laid to rest in the country’s Heroes Acre cemetery on Saturday following a state funeral attended by African leaders and foreign dignitaries.
item.dc:creator: SONJA SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia revels in Oval Office spectacle after Zelenskyy spars with Trump, Vance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's heated exchange with U.S. President Donald Trump has Russian leaders reveling in the spectacle, with one saying Kyiv got a "slap down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lebanon seizes $2.5M destined for Hezbollah after stopping man at airport in 'unprecedented’ bust
item.description: A man allegedly carrying $2.5 million in cash destined for Hezbollah reportedly was detained at Beirut’s airport in what an expert is calling an "unprecedented" bust.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 key takeaways from the Israeli military's report on what happened on Oct. 7
item.description: The Israeli military's assessment goes through the years, months and hours leading up to Hamas' brutal surprise attacks on Oct. 7, 2023. This includes intelligence and operational assessments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippines shark attack: 2 tourists killed at popular diving spot
item.description: Two tourists reportedly were killed by sharks in the Philippines near Verde Island after getting swept away by a current while diving at a popular location
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Embassy in Thailand warns Americans of 'violent retaliatory attacks' risk after Uyghurs deported to China
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Bangkok is warning Americans of potential "violent retaliatory attacks" after a group of Uyghurs were deported by Thailand to China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' condition improving but will remain 'guarded' until he is stable for several days: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is improving after spending the past two weeks hospitalized with double pneumonia, but the Vatican said his prognosis is "guarded" until he's stable for a few more days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's army admits failures on Oct. 7. Its probe of the attack could put pressure on Netanyahu
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — An investigation by the Israeli military has determined that Hamas was able to carry out the deadliest attack in Israeli history on Oct. 7, 2023, because the much more powerful Israeli army misjudged the militant group’s intentions and underestimated its capabilities.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands of Palestinians seek shelter after Israeli assaults across the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — When Israeli snipers took positions in her neighborhood, Haleemeh Zawaydeh knew her family needed to leave quickly. As the snipers' gunfire rang out, the 63-year-old matriarch said there was no time to pack as she and 14 other family members fled on foot.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, AREF TUFAHA and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico extradites dozens of cartel leaders and members to US, including drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
item.description: As Mexico and the U.S. negotiate trade tariffs, the Mexican government has handed over 29 cartel leaders and members who were wanted in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US wants to end Ukraine war with little involvement: Ex-ambassador
item.description: President Donald Trump has met with European leaders during the week to discuss ending the war quickly.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis' condition continues to improve as he receives oxygen therapy: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is improving as he undergoes high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said in a statement. The 88-year-old pontiff has been hospitalized for nearly two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: French surgeon accused of raping or abusing 299 people, mostly child patients
item.description: VANNES, France (AP) — A former surgeon went on trial in France on Monday for the alleged rape or sexual abuse of 299 victims, most of them children who were his patients, in what investigators and his own notebooks describe as a pattern of violence spanning over three decades.
item.dc:creator: JADE LE DELEY and NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
